consensus.text: A robust community enterprise means listening to people and adapting based on their needs.
There should be a "community economy" the way there is a creator economy, that is recognized and resourced
Not a must but nice to have: impact measurement for their groups, data collection. This could also be beneficial for grant applications
Practical workshops from experienced community builders would help me more than written guides or general resources.
Subsidized access to venues across the five boroughs would change what I can do more than direct funding.

divisive.text: simple systems (like CRM/community platforms) so we can focus on the 'how'—rather than the administrative 'what.' and spaces to do it
The platforms I rely on (Instagram, Eventbrite, Partiful) could change overnight and break my community
I would do this work full-time if I could financially.

representativeStatements.text: I would do this work full-time if I could financially.
Venues that benefit from hosting communities should offer builders better terms (lower minimums, off-peak access, reduced fees) not just charge market rates.
The city should treat community builders as a recognized category like artists or small businesses with permits, tax status, and access designed for them.
The platforms I rely on (Instagram, Eventbrite, Partiful) could change overnight and break my community
The administrative side of community work (insurance, permits, legal, payments) is or would be a real barrier for me.
simple systems (like CRM/community platforms) so we can focus on the 'how'—rather than the administrative 'what.' and spaces to do it

topStatements.label: Highest agreement
topStatements.text: There should be a "community economy" the way there is a creator economy, that is recognized and resourced

topStatements.label: Most divided
topStatements.text: The communities I build are partly a response to the failures of public institutions.

topStatements.label: Most engaged
topStatements.text: I've thought seriously about stopping my community work in the past year because of burnout
